original_hypothesis.title: HooksConfig.mergeFlags/encodeHooksDeploymentConfig bitfield slicing mistakes could silently enable/disable hooks, weakening access control or constraints
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: HooksConfig/HooksDeploymentConfig use nontrivial assembly slicing/shifting to merge optional and required flags. If any bit offset assumptions are wrong (e.g., flags not aligned to expected 16-bit window, incorrect shifts of 0x50/0x40), the merged hooks config used by markets may not match intended security posture. This can lead to hooks not being called (bypass constraints) or being called unexpectedly (operational DoS). The risk is increased by maintenance hazards (many constants, manual offsets, and multiple call constructors).
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a systemic configuration integrity risk: if hook enabling is wrong, all other hook logic is moot. Attackers exploit the resulting bypass by using market functions that should be hooked but are not.
deployment_assumptions: Market contract relies solely on HooksConfig flags to decide which hooks to call
Deployment populates HooksConfig values that are merged using these library functions (i.e., uses mergeFlags/config pattern)
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No Market contract code provided; need to verify it uses HooksConfig.mergeFlags output and respects the same bit layout.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Manual bit slicing: `shr(0x50, optionalFlags)`, `shr(0x40, requiredFlags)`, masks `0xffff`/`0xffff0000`

preconditions: There exists a mismatch between intended enabled hooks and actual merged bits
A market action depends on a hook being called for security (deposit/transfer/queueWithdrawal/parameter constraints)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Security depends on correct bitfield operations across multiple contracts and libraries; difficult to audit by inspection.
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Deployment-time config and market integration correctness.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time if mismerge causes hooks to not be invoked.

operational_questions: Do unit/invariant tests assert that each hook bit toggles the correct behavior end-to-end (Market -> Hooks)?
Is there a canonical spec for bit positions and are all usages consistent?
Do optional/required flags overlap or truncate unexpectedly due to 16-bit masking?
validation_requirements: Property tests enumerating all bit combinations and asserting Market calls expected hook functions
Cross-check bit positions against IHooks selectors used by Market
Simulate deployments and verify merged HooksConfig matches stored HookedMarket flags and behavior

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Find a market where expected hook is not actually called due to mismerged flags.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Perform the now-unhooked sensitive market action (e.g., deposit without access, parameter change outside constraints).
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Escalate impact depending on bypassed hook (access control bypass or solvency constraint bypass).
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Depends on existence of misconfiguration/bitfield bug.

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Indirect: may allow unprivileged actions to proceed without checks.
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Medium: either bypass of restrictions or unexpected reverts.
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium: requires redeploy/upgrade of markets or hooks (protocol is non-upgradable).

execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: Any time if bug exists; no ordering required.

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Markets where hooksAddress is set but expected hook calls are absent in traces; discrepancies between config flags and observed behavior.
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Access control not enforced despite flags set; or enforced despite flags cleared.

validation_result.impact_summary: No actual bitfield misalignment exists. All shifts and masks are consistent across encodeHooksDeploymentConfig, mergeFlags, optionalFlags, requiredFlags, mergeSharedFlags, and mergeAllFlags.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: I traced every bit position through the complete encode→merge pipeline. HooksConfig stores 11 flag bits at positions 95-85 (from the right). encodeHooksDeploymentConfig shifts optional flags right by 0x50 (80) to place them at bits 15-5, and shifts required flags right by 0x40 (64) to place them at bits 31-21 (masked by 0xffff0000). mergeFlags reverses this: it shifts the market's config right by 0x50 to align at bits 15-5, uses the HooksDeploymentConfig directly for optional (bits 15-5 match), shifts required right by 0x10 (16) to move bits 31-21 to bits 15-5, then computes (config & optional) | required, masks with 0xffff, and shifts back left by 0x50. All three flag sets are consistently aligned at bits 15-5 during the merge operation. The 5 lowest bits (4-0) are always zero for properly constructed configs, so the 0xffff mask introduces no junk. I verified with concrete values from the FixedTermLoanHooks constructor and confirmed the output matches expected semantics.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Every assembly block was traced with exact bit positions. encodeHooksDeploymentConfig (HooksConfig.sol): shr(0x50) moves flags from bits 95-85 to 15-5; and(0xffff) correctly captures them; shr(0x40) moves flags to 31-21; and(0xffff0000) captures them. mergeFlags: shr(0x50) on config aligns at 15-5; _optionalFlags bits 15-5 already aligned; shr(0x10) on flags moves required from 31-21 to 15-5. All three operands are correctly aligned for the (config & optional) | required computation. mergeSharedFlags and mergeAllFlags use shl(0xa0)/shr(0xa0) to isolate and operate on the flag bits, which is also correct.

calling_sequence: 1. Constructor calls encodeHooksConfig for optionalFlags and requiredFlags, placing flag bits at positions 95-85
2. Constructor calls encodeHooksDeploymentConfig(optionalFlags, requiredFlags) which packs optional at bits 15-5 and required at bits 31-21 of HooksDeploymentConfig
3. _onCreateMarket calls marketHooksConfig.mergeFlags(config) which aligns config flags (shr 0x50), optional (direct), and required (shr 0x10) all at bits 15-5
4. Merged result = (config & optional) | required, shifted back to bits 95-85 via shl(0x50)
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: No vulnerable path found. The bit alignment is consistent at every step: flags always occupy bits 95-85 in HooksConfig and bits 15-5 (optional) / 31-21 (required) in HooksDeploymentConfig. The merge correctly aligns all three operands before performing the logical operations.

deployment_assumptions_checked: Market contract relies solely on HooksConfig flags to decide which hooks to call
Deployment populates HooksConfig values that are merged using these library functions
config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts that exercise market creation were provided. The LibDeployment.sol is a generic deployment utility. However, both FixedTermLoanHooks and AccessControlHooks constructors call encodeHooksDeploymentConfig, and their _onCreateMarket functions call mergeFlags. The code itself is the authoritative source.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The hypothesized attack requires a bitfield misalignment bug that would cause hooks to be silently enabled or disabled. After exhaustive analysis of all bit operations, no such misalignment exists. The optional/required flag semantics work exactly as intended: required hooks are always enabled regardless of market config, optional hooks are only enabled if the market config also requests them. The mergeFlags function correctly implements (config & optional) | required with consistent bit alignment.
validation_result.impact_assessment: No impact. The bitfield operations are correct. The only cosmetic issue found (noted in background context) is onBorrow using RepayHook_Base_Size instead of BorrowHook_Base_Size, but both equal 0x0224 so there is no functional impact, and this is unrelated to the flag merging hypothesis.
recommended_mitigations: Add comprehensive unit tests for mergeFlags that enumerate all individual flag combinations to serve as regression tests
Replace RepayHook_Base_Size with BorrowHook_Base_Size in onBorrow to prevent future divergence (cosmetic fix)
Consider adding named constants for the shift amounts (0x50, 0x40, 0x10, 0xa0) to improve readability
validation_result.replication_plan: Write a Foundry test that: (1) creates HooksConfigs with each individual flag set, (2) encodes them into HooksDeploymentConfig, (3) merges with various market configs, (4) asserts each flag in the result matches the expected (config & optional) | required semantics. Include edge cases with all flags set, no flags set, and mismatched configs.
uncertainties: Market contract code is not in scope - we cannot verify it reads HooksConfig flags with the same bit layout, though the shared HooksConfig.sol library and readFlag function make this very likely correct
additional_testing_needed: End-to-end integration test verifying Market contract calls the correct hooks based on merged flags
Fuzz test on mergeFlags with random flag combinations verifying (config & optional) | required invariant
